title: Frontend Quality: Theming, i18n, Performance & Code Quality
note: Re-audited 2026-03-16. Original 62 tasks (T-001 to T-062) all completed by other teams. New task set created for the 7 remaining user stories identified in the exhaustive re-audit.

tasks.title: Fix LitoralMap.astro — replace 5 undefined CSS variable references
tasks.description:
Replace 3 undefined CSS variable references (5 occurrences) in `apps/web/src/components/destination/LitoralMap.astro`.

Broken → Correct mapping:
- Line 98–99: `var(--color-surface)` → `var(--color-card)` (2 occurrences in SVG gradient)
- Line 110: `var(--color-text-secondary)` → `var(--color-muted-foreground)` (river label text)
- Lines 194, 222: `var(--color-bg)` → `var(--color-background)` (2 occurrences, marker strokes and tooltip backgrounds)

Before changing, search the full codebase for each variable name to confirm no other component accidentally relied on these broken references.

Acceptance: `grep -r '--color-surface\|--color-text-secondary\|--color-bg' apps/web/src/components/destination/LitoralMap.astro` returns no matches.

tasks.title: Fix LitoralMap.astro — remove !important from opacity rule
tasks.description:
Remove the `opacity: 0 !important` at `apps/web/src/components/destination/LitoralMap.astro:290`. This is used to hide river SVG paths before scroll animation.

Replace with one of these alternatives:
1. Use a CSS class toggle via JavaScript (add/remove a class that sets `opacity: 0` without `!important`)
2. Use a data-attribute selector: `[data-hidden] { opacity: 0; }` applied via JS
3. Increase specificity of the selector to beat whatever rule is winning the cascade

Verify the scroll animation still works after removing `!important`.

Acceptance: `grep -n '!important' apps/web/src/components/destination/LitoralMap.astro` returns no matches.

tasks.title: Fix ShareButtons.client.tsx — add dark mode variant to Twitter/X button
tasks.description:
The Twitter/X button at `apps/web/src/components/shared/ShareButtons.client.tsx:191` uses `bg-black text-white`. In dark mode, `bg-black` on a dark page background makes the button invisible (fails WCAG AA 3:1 contrast for UI components).

Fix: Add `dark:bg-gray-700` (or `dark:bg-zinc-700`) to the button's class list, keeping `bg-black` for light mode.

Also verify WhatsApp (`bg-green-500`) and Facebook (`bg-blue-600`) buttons in dark mode — if they already pass 3:1 contrast ratio against `--color-background` in dark mode, no change needed for those.

Acceptance:
- Twitter/X button has a `dark:` Tailwind variant that provides sufficient background contrast in dark mode
- Light mode appearance unchanged
- Visual verification in both modes

tasks.title: Add aria-label to ReviewForm submit and cancel buttons
tasks.description:
The submit and cancel buttons in `apps/web/src/components/review/ReviewForm.client.tsx` at lines 382–396 have no `aria-label`. Screen readers announce them as generic "button" with no context.

Note: the star rating buttons (around line 262) are ALREADY fixed with `aria-label={tUi('accessibility.rateStars', ...)}` — do not touch those.

Fix: Add descriptive `aria-label` attributes to the submit and cancel buttons. Use the existing `tUi()` translation function — add keys to the i18n files if they don't already exist:
- Submit button: `aria-label={tUi('accessibility.submitReview')}` (or similar existing key)
- Cancel button: `aria-label={tUi('accessibility.cancelReview')}`

Acceptance:
- Both buttons have `aria-label` attributes
- `pnpm typecheck` passes
- axe-core reports no `button-name` violation on the review form page

tasks.title: Enforce label or aria-label at the TypeScript level in Input.tsx
tasks.description:
The generic input wrapper at `apps/admin/src/components/ui-wrapped/Input.tsx` (lines 102–122) accepts an optional `label` prop but does NOT enforce at the TypeScript level that either `label` or `aria-label` is provided.

Note: PriceRangeFilter inputs already use proper `<label>` elements with `htmlFor` — this task is about the admin Input.tsx wrapper only.

Fix: Update the component's props type to require either `label` or `aria-label` using a discriminated union or conditional type:
```typescript
type InputProps =
  | { label: string; 'aria-label'?: string }
  | { label?: never; 'aria-label': string }
```

This is a compile-time enforcement only — runtime behavior unchanged.

Acceptance:
- TypeScript prevents instantiating `<Input />` without either `label` or `aria-label`
- Existing usages of `<Input label="...">` still compile
- `pnpm typecheck` passes across the full admin app

tasks.title: Fix `any` types in EntityViewSection.tsx
tasks.description:
Replace `any` types in `apps/admin/src/components/entity-form/EntityViewSection.tsx` at lines 123 and 211.

The function `getNestedValue(obj: any, path: string): any` should be typed as:
- `obj: Record<string, unknown>`
- return type: `unknown` (caller is responsible for narrowing)

Update all call sites within the file to handle the `unknown` return type safely (type narrowing or type assertion with comment explaining why it's safe).

Acceptance:
- Zero `any` in this file
- `pnpm typecheck` passes

tasks.title: Fix `any` types in EntitlementGatedSection.tsx
tasks.description:
Replace `any` types in `apps/admin/src/components/entity-form/sections/EntitlementGatedSection.tsx` at lines 16 and 19.

- `children: any` → `children: React.ReactNode`
- `fallback?: any` → `fallback?: React.ReactNode`

These are standard React prop types — no complex type reasoning needed.

Acceptance:
- Zero `any` in this file
- `pnpm typecheck` passes

tasks.title: Fix `any` types in unflatten-values.utils.ts
tasks.description:
Replace `any` types in `apps/admin/src/components/entity-form/utils/unflatten-values.utils.ts` at lines 12 and 82.

`let current: any = obj` should be typed as `let current: Record<string, unknown>` or use a recursive type alias if the shape is self-referential.

Run existing tests for this utility after the change to confirm behavior is preserved.

Acceptance:
- Zero `any` in this file
- `pnpm typecheck` passes
- Existing unit tests pass

tasks.title: Fix `any` type for `route` property in entity-list/types.ts
tasks.description:
Replace `readonly route: any` at `apps/admin/src/components/entity-list/types.ts:216` with a proper TanStack Router type.

Use `AnyRoute` from `@tanstack/react-router` or the appropriate generic constraint. If `AnyRoute` causes import issues, use `import type { AnyRoute } from '@tanstack/react-router'`.

Acceptance:
- Zero `any` in this file for the `route` property
- `pnpm typecheck` passes across all entity-list usages

tasks.title: Fix `any` types in lib/cache/strategies/memoryOptimization.ts
tasks.description:
Replace `any` at `apps/admin/src/lib/cache/strategies/memoryOptimization.ts:325`.

The function `estimateQuerySize(query: any)` should accept `unknown` or a specific cache query shape type. If the function operates on arbitrary query objects, `unknown` with internal type narrowing is the correct pattern.

Acceptance:
- Zero `any` in this file
- `pnpm typecheck` passes

tasks.title: Resolve or file accommodations config TODOs (5 items)
tasks.description:
Review and action the 5 TODO comments in `apps/admin/src/features/accommodations/config/sections/`:

1. `amenities.consolidated.ts:15` — Category options for amenities not implemented
2. `basic-info.consolidated.ts:60` — RICH_TEXT field type missing
3. `gallery.consolidated.ts:15, 29` — Image/video type options missing (2 items)
4. `states-moderation.consolidated.ts:88` — DATE field type not implemented
5. `statistics.consolidated.ts:207, 221` — DATE fields not implemented (2 items)

Decision tree for each item:
- **Small fix (< 2h)**: Implement it here
- **Medium/large (> 1 day)**: Create a new task in the task system with clear description, add a comment referencing the task ID, remove the TODO
- **Obsolete**: Remove the TODO and add a comment explaining why it's no longer needed

Acceptance:
- Zero untracked TODO comments in these 5 files
- Each item is either implemented, has a new filed task, or the TODO is removed with explanation

tasks.title: Create composite index migration for billing tables
tasks.description:
Create a Drizzle migration adding 2 composite indexes for high-frequency billing query patterns:

1. **`billing_dunning_attempt`**: New index `(customer_id, result, created_at DESC)`
   - Note: an existing `(customer_id, result)` index is in migration 0015_bent_nekra.sql. DO NOT drop it — add the new composite separately.
   - Use `CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY` to avoid table locks

2. **`billing_notification_log`**: New index `(customer_id, type, created_at DESC)`
   - No existing index on this pattern
   - Use `CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY`

Generate the migration via: `pnpm db:generate`

Verify with `EXPLAIN ANALYZE` on a filtered+sorted query:
```sql
EXPLAIN ANALYZE SELECT * FROM billing_dunning_attempts
WHERE customer_id = '...' AND result = 'failed'
ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 20;
```
Expect: "Index Scan" not "Seq Scan".

Acceptance:
- Migration file created and applied
- `EXPLAIN ANALYZE` shows index scan for both target query patterns

tasks.title: Create composite index migration for entity tables (deleted_at, created_at)
tasks.description:
Create a Drizzle migration adding composite indexes `(deleted_at, created_at DESC)` for the 4 main entity tables:

- `accommodations`
- `destinations`
- `events`
- `posts`

These tables are frequently queried with `WHERE deleted_at IS NULL ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT N` — a pattern used in every admin list page. The composite index allows PostgreSQL to use an index scan for both the null filter and the sort.

Use `CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY` for each index.

Acceptance:
- 4 new composite indexes created via migration
- Migration applied without errors
- `EXPLAIN ANALYZE` on `WHERE deleted_at IS NULL ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 20` shows index scan

tasks.title: Full dark mode visual verification
tasks.description:
Manual visual verification pass in dark mode for all components changed in this spec plus spot-check of 10 other web components:

**Components changed in this spec:**
- `LitoralMap.astro` — province shapes, river labels, markers, tooltips (light + dark)
- `ShareButtons.client.tsx` — all 3 social buttons visible in dark mode

**Spot-check (10 other components):**
- Hero section
- Accommodation card
- Destination card
- Search/filter bar
- Navigation header
- Footer
- Review card
- Event card
- Error pages (404, 500)
- Price range filter

For each: toggle `data-theme="dark"` on `<html>` and visually confirm no transparent, invisible, or unreadable elements.

Acceptance:
- Manual verification checklist in spec.md is fully checked
- No new dark mode regressions found

tasks.title: Accessibility audit with axe-core on affected pages
tasks.description:
Run axe-core accessibility audit on the pages affected by US-A01 changes:

1. Accommodation detail page (contains ReviewForm and ShareButtons)
2. Search results page (contains PriceRangeFilter)

Method: Use the browser axe DevTools extension or run `@axe-core/playwright` if E2E tests exist.

Focus on:
- `button-name` violations (ReviewForm buttons)
- `label` violations (form inputs)
- `color-contrast` violations (ShareButtons in dark mode)

Acceptance:
- Zero `button-name` violations on review form
- Zero `label` violations on search filters
- Zero `color-contrast` violations on ShareButtons (both modes)

tasks.title: Final quality gate — typecheck, lint, test
tasks.description:
Run the full quality gate before closing SPEC-022:

```bash
pnpm typecheck   # must pass with zero errors
pnpm lint        # must pass with zero errors
pnpm test        # must pass with ≥90% coverage
```

Fix any failures before marking the spec as completed.

Acceptance:
- All three commands exit with code 0
- Coverage report shows ≥90% across modified files
